`reset_peak_memory_stats`/`max_memory_allocated`, sweeps batch size for headroom (stopping at the
first OOM), and publishes a self-validating report. `build_smoke_report` is **stdlib-pure** (the
hardware block is passed in, not probed) so the bare-CI tier can prove the gate arithmetic without
torch. `slurm/p1/lora_smoke.sbatch` mirrors the accepted `rinalmo_throughput.sbatch` shape
(flock singleton, stale-marker clear *before* conda, node-local `/tmp` HF cache + EXIT trap,
DONE-gate on a self-validated report). **Not yet run — the §9.3 submit-ack is pending.**

**Outputs.** `src/tbox_finder/train/lora_harness.py` (+P1-16) · `slurm/p1/lora_smoke.sbatch` ·
`tests/ml/test_lora_smoke.py` (3 tiers). `reports/p1/lora_vram_smoke.json` + the throwaway LoRA
adapter are the **SLURM run's** outputs and do not exist yet.

**Validation.** Authoring gate: **75 P1-16 tests green** (72 bare stdlib + 3 torch/peft under
`tbox-ml-rna`); full bare suite **931 passed**, no regression (P1-15's 107 included); ruff 0.15.15
+ black 25.11.0 clean; `snakemake --lint` clean; `bash -n` clean. The load-bearing local result: **one real LoRA step
runs end-to-end through a real (tiny) RiNALMo with gradient checkpointing and every LoRA
parameter receives a finite gradient** — the frozen-base + gradient-checkpointing failure mode
(checkpointed segments receive inputs with `requires_grad=False` ⇒ the step "succeeds" while
training nothing) is measured, not assumed. The **binding** gate (peak VRAM ≤ 16 GB on real
sm_86) is the SLURM run's and is **not claimed here**.

**Decisions.** (1) **The 16 GB budget is the only pinned number** — PRD §10.2 condition (b) /
§10.3 / §15 + ADR-0002 D6(b). Batch size, sequence length, step count and LR are **pinned
nowhere** (verified across PRD §10.2/§10.3/§11/§15 and every ADR-0002 D/A), so they are recorded
as implementer choices with `config.pinned=false` and a validator clause that rejects any report
claiming otherwise — the ADR-0002 A5 `atol` precedent. (2) **Gate on batch 1**: §10.2 (b) asks
*feasibility*, and a larger batch is a throughput preference P3 can trade for gradient
accumulation; the sweep is reported as headroom and is explicitly **not** a gate (an OOM at
batch 16 is a budgeting fact, not a §10.2 failure). (3) **§10.3 honesty — the loss is a
placeholder** (mean-squared activation over random RNA), stated not glossed: peak VRAM and step
latency are set by the 650 M-param encoder's activation/gradient/optimizer footprint at a given
(batch, length), and a P3 head is a `Linear(1280 → k)` beside it — but this is **not** a T-box
result, a convergence claim, or an LR finding, and `validate_smoke_report` refuses any artifact
that says otherwise. (4) **The carried P1-15 handoff is closed here**: P1-15 selected
`flash_attention_2` on an *import*-level sm_86 confirmation (A5 carries **no** FA-2 forward — its
only forward-parity tests are the Stage-1 selective-scan/causal-conv1d kernels) and recorded
`forward_verified_on_sm86:false`. imp.md's P1-16 gate never mentions attention, but this is the
last step before an FA-2-selecting config reaches P3, so a `gate.attention_forward_verified`
clause was **added**: the run loads under the selected backend, and a forward failure falls back
to SDPA (so the VRAM number still lands) while failing the gate — a §7 stop-and-ask, **not** an
auto-flip, because the selection lives in a committed artifact mirrored by
`conf/train/lora_stage2.yaml`. (5) On a VRAM miss the budget is **not weakenable** — §7
stop-and-ask, escalate to FSDP `FULL_SHARD` or the §10.2 RNA-FM consideration (the A7/A9
precedent). context7 §3.3: `/huggingface/peft` + `/huggingface/transformers` — the load-bearing
read being that `gradient_checkpointing_enable()` defaults to `use_reentrant=False` and
auto-calls `enable_input_require_grads()` when `main_input_name == "input_ids"` (RiNALMo's is —
verified by introspection, not assumed). Cross-link **PRD §10.2/§10.3/§11/§15**, **ADR-0002
D3/D5/D6(b)/A2 K1/A5/A9**.

**Pre-commit adversarial 6-lens review** (correctness / PRD-ADR-faithfulness / test-adequacy /
scientific-honesty / slurm-safety / reuse → adversarial refute; 40 agents) → **34 raw findings, 6
confirmed / 28 refuted**, all six fixed before push. All six were **one root cause**, and it is the
same lesson P1-15 learned when its `lora_config_exact` proved tautological:
`overall_pass = all(clauses)` catches a clause flipped **false** (it then contradicts a true
`overall_pass`) but **structurally cannot catch a clause fabricated true** — an all-true gate is
self-consistent whatever the evidence says. Only `peak_vram_within_budget` was re-derived; the
other seven were taken on faith. Fixed by giving each clause a **single shared derivation**
(`_is_sm86`, `_bf16_and_ckpt`, `_num`) used by **both** `build_smoke_report` and
`validate_smoke_report`, so the artifact must equal what the evidence implies: (1) `wrap` +
`hardware` added to `_SMOKE_BLOCKS` (a block the validator never required is a block a report can
omit); (2) `gate.measured_on_sm86` re-derived from `hardware.cuda_capability` — an artifact
flagged `is_sm86:true` beside an sm_89 capability previously validated clean; (3)
`gate.bf16_and_gradient_checkpointing` re-derived from the `wrap` that ran — the validator had
only checked the `lora` block, which `build_smoke_report` **hardcoded from the module constants**,
so it compared the pins to themselves (a fp32/checkpointing-off run would still have certified
§10.3); (4) `gate.fits_on_device` re-derived from peak vs the recorded card size; (5)
`gate.step_runs_end_to_end` + `gate.steps_per_sec_reported` re-derived from `measured_smoke.gate_run`
(a missing `steps_per_sec`, or the string `"fast"`, previously validated clean — and steps/sec is an
explicit imp.md clause); (6) a **`lora_config_exact` clause added** off `wrap.applied_lora`, fail-closed,
so the VRAM number cannot be measured under one config while the report certifies another. Also
fixed, and a live landmine for the next session: `TBOX_REQUIRE_LORA_SMOKE` guarded **both** the
pure-JSON Tier 3 **and** the torch tier through a shared `_fail_or_skip`, so the moment the result
commit wired it into CI (as this module's docstring promises) the torch tier would have **failed on
every bare-CI run** — torch is not installed there. Split into `TBOX_REQUIRE_LORA_TORCH` for the
torch tier; verified by simulating the CI job (`env -u CUDA_HOME TBOX_REQUIRE_LORA_SMOKE=1` → torch
tier skips, Tier 3 correctly fails on the absent report). The 28 refuted included several plausible
misreads worth noting as *not* defects: `max_memory_allocated` excluding the CUDA context (it is the
right instrument for a *comparative* budget claim), the sbatch EXIT trap deleting the adapter
(§9.2 mandates node-local build + cleanup, and imp.md calls the adapter throwaway), and a claimed
inability to record a VRAM miss (unreachable at batch 1 — see below).

**One honest limitation, recorded not hidden (§10.3).** On a 15.6 GiB A4000 a peak *above* 16 GiB is
physically unreachable — the allocator OOMs first — so `peak_vram_within_budget` can essentially
never fail on this hardware. The real §10.2 (b) failure signature is an **OOM in the gate run**,
which propagates out of `run_vram_smoke`, leaves **no DONE marker**, and becomes a §9.3
stop-and-ask rather than a fabricated report. The 16 GB clause is retained because it is the PRD's
pin and the number a reviewer checks; `gate.fits_on_device` is the clause that carries the physical
constraint. A local **advisory** de-risk (NOT the §10.3 sm_86 confirmation, and recorded in no
artifact): FA-2 forwards cleanly through a tiny RiNALMo on the sm_89 laptop, so the
`attention_bias`/4D-mask concern the P1-15 handoff flagged is unlikely to sink the cluster run.

**Commit.** squash **`4e34dee`** on `main` (PR [#49](https://github.com/bioedca/tbox-finder/pull/49)).

## Step P1-16 (result) — 2026-07-15: LoRA VRAM smoke MEASURED — 1.484 GiB ≤ 16 GB; FA-2 forward CONFIRMED on sm_86

**Inputs.** The authored harness at `2aba5f5`; SLURM job **521** on a cluster RTX A4000
(sm_86, cap `[8,6]`, 15.602 GiB usable, driver 590.48.01), env `tbox-ml-rna`; the A9
parity-confirmed `multimolecule/rinalmo-giga` @`2a71f6f9…`; a seeded synthetic 350-nt RNA
batch. Elapsed ~135 s.

**Process.** §9.3 protocol complete: authoring commit → push [bioedca] → cluster synced to
`2aba5f5` → `sbatch --test-only` PASS (job 520, both gpu nodes up) → **user submit-ack** →
job 521 → artifact-verify (`squeue` empty + `lora_vram_smoke.DONE` + a self-validated
report; `.err` = only the expected `lm_head.* UNEXPECTED` load notes [we load the bare
encoder from a checkpoint carrying the pretraining MLM head], the FA-2 fp32→bf16 cast note,
and transformers' `use_cache=True` → `False` auto-fix under gradient checkpointing — **no
tracebacks**) → rsync → re-validated locally (`validate_smoke_report` → no errors).

**Outputs.** Measured **`reports/p1/lora_vram_smoke.json`** (git-tracked) +
`.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`TBOX_REQUIRE_LORA_SMOKE: "1"` — the Tier-3 gate goes live now
that the report exists). The throwaway LoRA adapter was written to node-local
`/tmp/ecc1695-521/lora_adapter` and died with the EXIT trap, as designed.

**Validation. GATE PASSED — all 10 clauses, `overall_pass=true`, every clause re-derived
from its recorded evidence.** **Peak VRAM at the gate config (batch 1, 350 nt, bf16 +
gradient checkpointing) = 1.484 GiB of 15.602** — **9.5 % of the card**, vs the pinned
16 GB budget. **PRD §10.2 condition (b)'s VRAM half is answered: Stage-2 LoRA fine-tuning
fits one A4000 with ~10× headroom; the RNA-FM swap is NOT triggered on VRAM.** Headroom
sweep (reported, never gated): batch {1: 1.484, 2: 1.558, 4: 1.706, 8: 2.014, 16: 2.647} GiB
— **no OOM anywhere**, `largest_fitting_batch=16`, `oom_at_batch=null`. steps/sec (reported,
**not** adjudicated — PRD §10.2 freezes the latency decision to the P5 sizing gate): gate
run **2.70 steps/s** (step_ms mean 370.4); sweep {1: 3.27, 2: 3.23, 4: 1.91, 8: 1.01,
16: 0.52} steps/s. Mechanics: **462/462 LoRA params received finite gradients** (231 adapted
modules × A+B — internally consistent), `base_frozen=true`, **231/231 eligible `nn.Linear`
adapted** (`all_linear_fully_covered`), applied config read off the model = **r=16 / α=32 /
dropout=0.05** exactly, **12,840,432 / 662,079,483 trainable (1.9394 %)** — *identical* to
P1-15's dry-run figures, an independent cross-check that the GPU run wrapped the same model
the CPU dry-run did. Adapter dtypes measured `torch.float32` over the bf16 base (PEFT's
`autocast_adapter_dtype` default — recorded, not assumed). Stack: torch 2.7.1+cu128 /
transformers 5.13.0 / multimolecule 0.1.0 / peft 0.18.1 / accelerate 1.12.0 / deepspeed
0.18.5.

**Decisions.** (1) **The carried P1-15 handoff is CLOSED, positively.** `flash_attention_2`
was **loaded and forwarded through RiNALMo on a real sm_86 A4000** — `used == selected ==
flash_attention_2`, `forward_verified_on_sm86: true`, `forward_error: null`. The
`attention_bias`/4D-mask failure mode the handoff feared did not materialise; ADR-0002 A2
K1's ImportError caveat is moot (the wheel is present and works). **No §7 stop, no SDPA
flip, no ADR amendment** — the P1-15 selection stands and is now forward-verified rather
than import-inferred. P1-15's own artifact correctly keeps `forward_verified_on_sm86:false`
(it records what P1-15 knew); P1-16's report carries the verification, which is why the two
validators are deliberately separate. (2) **PRD §10.3's "frozen base ≈1.3 GB" arithmetic
estimate is now MEASURED and confirmed**: 650 M × 2 bytes ≈ 1.3 GB, and the measured 1.484
GiB peak is that base plus fp32 adapter grads (~51 MB), AdamW states (~103 MB) and
checkpointed activations — the estimate was sound, and it is no longer an estimate (this
closes the P1-15 note that flagged it as unmeasured). (3) **The 16 GB clause passed by a
factor of ~10, and the honest reading is that it could hardly have failed**: on a 15.6 GiB
card an over-16 GiB peak is unreachable (the allocator OOMs first), so the clause is a
formality on this hardware and the real condition-(b) risk was always an OOM — which did not
occur at any swept batch. The informative numbers are the *margin* (1.484 of 15.602) and the
*absence of OOM through batch 16*, not the boolean. Stated rather than presented as a
hard-won pass (§10.3). (4) **Still not science**: random RNA, placeholder loss,
`config.is_science=false` — this establishes that Stage-2 *can* be fine-tuned on one A4000,
not that it *learns* anything. P3 owns that. **Commit.** squash **`4e34dee`** on `main` (PR [#49](https://github.com/bioedca/tbox-finder/pull/49)).
Cross-link **PRD §10.2 (b)/§10.3/§11/§15**, **ADR-0002 D3/D5/D6(b)/A2 K1/A5/A9**.
